topic.what: Proximity gaps and list-decoding bounds for Reed-Solomon codes near capacity
topic.how: Construct explicit counterexamples using vanishing polynomials on multiplicative subgroups of prime fields with parameters n=2^b, p=Theta(n^beta)
topic.why_matters: Disproves key conjectures used in FRI proof systems and IOP security; affects parameters of zero-knowledge proof systems like Ethereum's

characteristics.what_it_measures: Maximum number of codewords within distance delta-eta of a word
characteristics.vs_baseline: Exponential improvement over conjectured poly(n) bound
characteristics.evidence: Theorem 1 Item 1: |{c in C : Delta(c,u) <= delta*-eta}| >= 2^{c/eta}
characteristics.context: Conjecture 2 predicts poly(n) list sizes; actual is superpolynomial (n^tau)

characteristics.what_itasures: Number of theta-close points on line not entirely in theta-neighborhood
characteristics.evidence: Theorem 1 Item 2: a(delta*-2eta) >= 2^{c/eta} for eta=Theta(1/log n)
characteristics.context: Shows proximity gaps fail at eta=Theta(1/log n) scale; n^tau vs poly(n) expectation

characteristics.what_it_measures: Relative distance of constructed RS code
characteristics.evidence: delta* = delta+o(1/log n) for code with rate rho
characteristics.context: Code operates near capacity (relative distance theta = delta-eta)

characteristics.what_it_measures: Gap from capacity where failure occurs
characteristics.vs_baseline: Previous known failures at Elias radius; this shows earlier failure
characteristics.evidence: eta = c/(tau log n) with c in (0,1) constant depending on delta*
characteristics.context: Minimum eta in conjecture is 1/n; paper shows failure at much larger eta=Theta(1/log n)

good_for: understanding RS code limitations near capacity
not_for: construction of good IOPs near capacity
codes over general fields (binary or general field positive results exist)
requires: RS codes over multiplicative subgroups of prime fields
Large field size relative to code length (p=Theta(n^beta) with beta > tau+1)

novelty: First counterexample to proximity gap Conjecture 1 near capacity for prime field subgroups
Shows list size n^tau at eta=Theta(1/log n) (invalidates Conjecture 2)
New additive-combinatorics lemma on sums of roots of unity
Constructive vanishing polynomial approach via large cosets
open_problems: Can beta be reduced to 1 via Montgomery conjecture?
Characterize which subgroups have good/bad proximity gaps
Positive results for specific structured subgroups
Improved list-decoding bounds for other code families
